evals.description: Fixed synthetic auth handler diff — must select a security hotspot and produce high/critical findings. No network required, fully reproducible.
Docs-majority diff (under 100 lines) must cap hotspots at 3 and select docs-quality lens on the hotspot.
evals.prompt:
Review this diff:

diff --git a/README.md b/README.md
index abc..def 100644
--- a/README.md
+++ b/README.md
@@ -1,3 +1,5 @@
 # My Project
 
-Install with npm.
+## Installation
+
+Install with `npm install my-project`. Run `npm start` to launch.
diff --git a/docs/guide.md b/docs/guide.md
new file mode 100644
--- /dev/null
+++ b/docs/guide.md
@@ -0,0 +1,5 @@
+# Guide
+
+See README for setup.
+
+Configuration options are listed below.

evals.description: Vague opinion request with no diff or code reference must NOT trigger the skill pipeline.
evals.prompt: What do you think of these changes?

evals.description: Two hotspot subagents producing the same fingerprint must result in one surviving candidate (duplicate-in-run) not two selected candidates.
evals.prompt: Review PR #42 [precondition: two hotspot subagents both return a candidate with fingerprint 'auth-boundary-regression|updateUser|authorization-preserved|public-endpoint']

evals.description: A diff under 100 changed lines must produce at most 3 hotspots.
evals.prompt:
Review this diff:

diff --git a/config.yaml b/config.yaml
--- a/config.yaml
+++ b/config.yaml
@@ -1,3 +1,5 @@
 database:
   host: localhost
+  password: secret123
+  token: abc
   port: 5432

expected_output.note: hotspot_count_max: 3 is the key assertion; surfaced_count_max: 5 allows for the security finding the diff contains

evals.description: A candidate with severity=high but confidence_local=0.60 must be downgraded to medium by Phase 6 confidence gates (high requires confidence_local >= 0.70).

expected_output.note: Precondition: the auth-boundary hotspot subagent returns a finding with severity=high and confidence_local=0.60. Phase 6 confidence gate must downgrade it.

evals.description: All selected candidates must have priority_score > 0 in SQLite after Phase 7 completes. This catches the bug where the Phase 7 UPDATE filters on detection_state='selected' before that column is written to SQLite.

expected_output.note: Query: SELECT count(*) FROM candidate_findings WHERE detection_state='selected' AND priority_score = 0; must return 0.
